name: Dixie II+
description: Full-featured analog triangle-core VCO/LFO. Five simultaneous waveform outputs plus square sub, dual FM with selectable linear/exponential, PWM, hard sync and direction-flip sync.

controls.name: OCTAVE
controls.description: 8-position slide switch sets coarse tuning in octave steps.
controls.detail: 8 positions · 1 octave per step · highest positions can clip LFO upper limit

controls.name: EXP/LIN
controls.description: Selects response curve of the FM1 input.
controls.detail: EXP: musical FM · LIN: through-zero-style timbral FM

controls.name: FINE
controls.description: Fine tuning knob. Range depends on VCO/LFO mode.

controls.name: VCO/LFO
controls.description: Switches between audio-rate and low-frequency operation.
controls.detail: VCO: 20 Hz–~60 kHz · LFO: several minutes up to ~1 kHz

controls.name: FM1 Attenuator
controls.description: Scales depth of the FM1 input before summing.
controls.detail: CCW: zero · CW: unity · unipolar attenuator

controls.name: PWM Attenuator
controls.description: Scales depth of the PWM CV input.
controls.detail: CCW: zero · CW: unity · unipolar attenuator

controls.name: PULSE WIDTH
controls.description: Sets base pulse duty cycle of the PULSE output, summed with PWM input.
controls.detail: CCW: 10% · noon: 50% square · CW: 90% · can hit 0%/100% for silence

inputs.name: FM1
inputs.description: Frequency modulation input; curve set by EXP/LIN switch and scaled by FM1 attenuator

inputs.name: FM2
inputs.description: Dedicated exponential frequency modulation input (no attenuator)

inputs.name: PWM
inputs.description: Pulse-width modulation input summed with PULSE WIDTH knob

inputs.name: 1V/OCT
inputs.description: Primary pitch control input, precision tracking

inputs.name: SYNC
inputs.description: Hard sync: waveform resets to zero on each zero-crossing of incoming signal

inputs.name: FLIP
inputs.description: Soft sync alternative: flips direction of triangle-core travel on zero-crossings instead of resetting

outputs.name: PULSE
outputs.description: Pulse wave with width set by PULSE WIDTH knob + PWM input

outputs.name: SUB
outputs.description: Square sub-oscillator, 50% duty cycle one octave below primary

behaviors.name: Simultaneous Waveforms
behaviors.description: All six outputs are live at once. Patch triangle to the filter while sending pulse to a ring mod and sub to the bass path — one VCO fans out into parallel voices.

behaviors.name: Hard Sync vs Flip Sync
behaviors.description: SYNC resets the core to zero, producing the classic hard-sync sweep timbre. FLIP reverses direction of travel instead, yielding softer, less phase-locked harmonics — useful for pseudo-linear-FM effects without losing pitch.

behaviors.name: Linear FM Mode
behaviors.description: FM1 becomes linear-response FM. Pairs with an AC-coupled audio-rate modulator for DX-style bell and metallic tones while 1V/OCT still tracks cleanly at the pitch input.

behaviors.name: LFO Mode
behaviors.description: Frequency range drops to LFO territory. All six outputs still live, so one module provides sine, triangle, zig-zag, saw, PWM pulse and sub-octave square LFOs simultaneously.

behaviors.name: PWM Range
behaviors.description: Knob and CV sum. At duty-cycle extremes (0% or 100%) the pulse goes silent — useful for envelope-style gating off the PWM jack or as a hard mute trick.

patchIdeas.name: Classic hard-sync lead
patchIdeas.patch: Master VCO into Dixie SYNC · sweep Dixie pitch with envelope · take saw output · sharp timbral sweeps tracking keyboard pitch of the master
patchIdeas.diagram:
Dixie II+:
* VCO mode · OCT 4

- Master VCO (Saw) -> Dixie II+ (SYNC)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Master VCO (V/Oct)
- Envelope (Out) >> Dixie II+ (FM2)
- Dixie II+ (SAW) -> Filter (In)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Dixie II+ (1V/OCT) // hard-sync sweep

patchIdeas.name: Linear-FM bell
patchIdeas.patch: Modulator VCO sine → Dixie FM1 · EXP/LIN set to LIN · FM1 attenuator ~noon · take SINE output · produces clean DX-style bells that still track 1V/oct
patchIdeas.diagram:
Dixie II+:
* VCO mode · EXP/LIN=LIN · FM1 atten noon

- Modulator VCO (Sine) -> Dixie II+ (FM1)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Dixie II+ (1V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Modulator VCO (V/Oct)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Dixie II+ (SINE) -> VCA (In) // linear FM bell

patchIdeas.name: Sub-augmented bass
patchIdeas.patch: Saw to filter for main voice · SUB mixed underneath at equal level · PULSE width narrowed for click transient · thick square-octave-down bass with percussive attack
patchIdeas.diagram:
Dixie II+:
* VCO mode · PW ~11 o'clock

- Dixie II+ (SAW) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Dixie II+ (SUB) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Dixie II+ (PULSE) -> Mixer (In 3)
- Mixer (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Dixie II+ (1V/OCT) // fat bass + sub + click

patchIdeas.name: Six-output LFO bank
patchIdeas.patch: VCO/LFO switch to LFO · OCT low · use all six outputs as independent LFOs: sine to filter, saw to pan, zig-zag to VCA, pulse as clock divider, sub as half-rate trigger source
patchIdeas.diagram:
Dixie II+:
* LFO mode · OCT 1 · FINE 12 o'clock

- Dixie II+ (SINE) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Dixie II+ (SAW) >> Panner (CV)
- Dixie II+ (ZIG-ZAG) >> VCA (CV)
- Dixie II+ (PULSE) c> Clock Divider (In)
- Dixie II+ (SUB) t> Envelope (Trig) // one module, six LFOs

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate PWM squelch
patchIdeas.patch: Audio-rate LFO or second VCO sine into PWM · PWM attenuator ~2 o'clock · PULSE output sweeps duty cycle at audio rate · produces syncopated, hollow timbres without a filter
patchIdeas.diagram:
Dixie II+:
* VCO mode · PW noon · PWM atten 2 o'clock

- Modulator VCO (Sine) >> Dixie II+ (PWM)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Dixie II+ (1V/OCT)
- Dixie II+ (PULSE) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV) // audio-rate PWM hollow timbre

patchIdeas.name: Flip-sync soft FM
patchIdeas.patch: Modulator saw into FLIP input · produces inharmonic overtones without phase-locking to a single hard-sync edge · softer, more vowel-like than SYNC · pair with BPF for formant sweeps
patchIdeas.diagram:
Dixie II+:
* VCO mode · OCT 4

- Modulator VCO (Saw) -> Dixie II+ (FLIP)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Dixie II+ (1V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Modulator VCO (V/Oct)
- Dixie II+ (TRIANGLE) -> BPF (In)
- LFO (Out) >> BPF (Cutoff) // flip-sync formant sweeps
